architecture.summary: TinyNS is a platform-aware neural-symbolic architecture search framework that jointly optimizes symbolic and neural operators for extreme-edge deployment. The system uses a gradient-free Bayesian optimizer to search over a discontinuous, conditional space of model configurations (5 neurosymbolic types), generating microcontroller code that interleaves symbolic rules/physics constraints with neural components. During optimization, TinyNS communicates with target hardware to ensure resource feasibility (memory, compute, latency), guaranteeing deployability before code generation.
trained_components: neural operators (learned weights within selected neurosymbolic model types)

biblio.title: TinyNS: Platform-aware Neurosymbolic Auto Tiny Machine Learning
biblio.venue: ACM Transactions on Embedded Computing Systems

claims.statement: TinyNS provides recipes and parsers to automatically write microcontroller code for five types of neurosymbolic models, combining the context awareness and integrity of symbolic techniques with the robustness and performance of machine learning models.

TinyNS uses a fast, gradient-free, black-box Bayesian optimizer over discontinuous, conditional, numeric, and categorical search spaces to find the best synergy of symbolic code and neural networks within the hardware resource budget.
To guarantee deployability, TinyNS talks to the target hardware during the optimization process.

baseline.name: purely neural approaches; purely symbolic approaches

claims.statement: In all use cases, TinyNS outperforms purely neural or purely symbolic approaches while guaranteeing execution on real hardware.
